analysis.reasoning: Core message: A Latvian infectious disease expert endorses vaccination (“I did it because this is the way”) with hashtags #GetTheVaccine and #StrongerTogether. No explicit mention of the EU or EU policies. Evaluation per narrative: EU1 (EU successful story) – not referenced; the tweet is about vaccination, not EU history or achievements. EU2 (EU united) – “StrongerTogether” implies solidarity but without tying it to the EU or inter-member unity; no explicit EU framing, so not applicable. EU3 (EU useful for its citizens) – vaccination endorsement could align with public health, but there is no mention of EU policy, programs, or benefits attributable to the EU, so not applicable. EU4 (EU avant‑garde actor) – no reference to EU leadership or regulation. EU5 (EU champion of fair causes) – unrelated. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – no global/EU cooperation framing. Conclusion: The tweet promotes vaccination/solidarity generally, not any specific EU narrative.
